issues.code:
1 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
2 from http import HTTPStatus

issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called

issues.code:
1 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
2 from http import HTTPStatus
3 from typing import Optional
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
issues.judge_reason: Importing xml.etree.ElementTree without defusedxml is flagged because the entire module is unsafe for untrusted XML. This is the same root cause as B314 (the unsafe parsing on line 14), but at the import level. Since the underlying vulnerability is the same (unsafe XML parsing leading to XXE), this is a duplicate of bandit:B314#line14.

issues.code:
13         try:
14             root = ET.fromstring(request_body)
15         except ET.ParseError as e:
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: The code uses ET.fromstring on untrusted XML input without defusedxml or any other protection against XXE. This is a well-known vulnerability: an attacker can define external entities in the XML to read arbitrary files or cause denial of service. The code does not call defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() or use defusedxml.ElementTree.
